comment: Exact lexical match on class label Person.
Exact lexical match on class label Document.
Exact lexical match on Review; both are document-like review artifacts.
Same label Session, but modeled differently: EKAW session is a scientific event, IASTED session is a lecture/conference activity.
Same label Tutorial, but EKAW models it as an individual presentation/event while IASTED models it as a lecture.
Presenter in EKAW is semantically close to Speaker in IASTED as both denote conference participants who present.
Near-exact lexical match differing only in underscore/case; both denote session chairs.
EKAW Student is broader than IASTED Student_lecturer, which is a student subtype of lecturer/author.
Both denote banquet-style social events associated with a conference.
Both represent optional trip/tour activities associated with the conference context.
EKAW Possible_Reviewer is broader than IASTED Reviewer, which is defined as someone who gives a review.
Both denote authors of submitted/presented papers, though IASTED Author is also a Speaker subtype.
Location in EKAW is semantically close to Place in IASTED as generic spatial entities.
Related via review relation semantics, but not equivalent: EKAW hasReview links paper to review, while IASTED give links person to item/activity.
Both properties connect a review/document to its writer; IASTED property is more generic for items.
Inverse-style naming difference but same core authored-by semantics between document/item and person.
EKAW authorOf is inverse of writtenBy; IASTED write is inverse of is_writen_by, yielding close semantic correspondence.
Both indicate where an activity/event occurs, though domain/range differ somewhat.
Reviewer participation is related to review authorship, but properties are not directly equivalent.
Lexically related through research semantics, but EKAW models a topic while IASTED models an activity class.
